channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Bellingham, kits and team names: What’s changed in The Hundred for 2026?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: As The Hundred returns for 2026 - live on Sky Sports from Tuesday July 21 to Sunday August 16 - we look at what has changed for the ECB's 100-ball competition…

item.title: Kane backs Tuchel for England success and aims for 2030 World Cup spot
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: England captain Harry Kane has backed Thomas Tuchel to succeed as England head coach amid criticism of his tactics in the World Cup semi-final defeat over Argentina.

item.title: Spain lift second World Cup title | The Final Verdict
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Sky Sports football correspondent Rob Dorsett analyses Spain's victory over Argentina in the World Cup final - plus the first half-time show in the history of the tournament.

item.title: Oluyede: Tinubu’s 2027 Re-election Should Not Be Taken For Granted
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) candidate in the 2026 Ekiti State governorship election, Oluwole Oluyede, has warned that President Bola Tinubu's re-election in 2027 should not be taken for granted, despite the All Progressives Congress (APC)'s victory in the state's governorship poll. The post O

item.title: World Cup 2026: Football Won —Kroos Taunts Argentina After Defeat To Spain
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Former Germany international Toni Kroos has taunted Argentina, after they lost to Spain in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final on Sunday, beIN Sports reports. Spain were crowned world champions for the second time, and the first since 2010, as Ferran Torres’ 106th-minute winner proved to be the diff

item.title: “Your personal example continues to inspire many,” Ohanaeze hails Obi at 65
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: While lauding the former Anambra governor's contributions to national development, the group described Obi as a disciplined leader whose personal example continues to inspire many Nigerians, especially the youth.

item.title: Dollar to Naira exchange rate today, July 20, 2026
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The naira traded relatively stable against the United States dollar at the official Nigerian Foreign Exchange Market (NFEM) on Monday, July 20, 2026, while the parallel market continued to quote the greenback at a premium. The post Dollar to Naira exchange rate today, July 20, 2026 appeared first on

item.title: FULL LIST: Mbappe leads Pele, Messi, others on World Cup’s all-time scorers chart
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kylian Mbappe has surpassed Pele and Messi to become the World Cup’s all-time leading scorer with 22 goals. See the full list of top scorers.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The governorship candidate of the APC for the August 15 election in Osun State, Bola Oyebamiji, has assured residents that, if elected, he will make the state safe for business and create opportunities for citizens to prosper. The post Oyebamiji meets Osun Muslim community, pledges fairness appeared

item.title: ‘I can’t date, marry man who wants me to cook regularly’ – Mercy Eke
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Big Brother Naija winner, Mercy Eke has sparked reactions after sharing her opinion on relationship and domestic chores. The reality star said she can’t marry or date a man who wants her to cook for him regularly.  She emphasised that she can only cook for her man once in a while w

item.title: Burna Boy, Shakira thrill fans with unforgettable performance in World Cup halftime show
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigerian music superstar Burna Boy and Colombian global icon Shakira delivered a memorable performance during FIFA's inaugural World Cup final halftime show on Sunday, entertaining millions of football fans worldwide as Spain defeated Argentina to claim the 2026 FIFA World Cup title. The post Burna

item.title: World Cup final: What Argentina coach, Scaloni said about Messi after Spain defeat
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina coach, Lionel Scaloni, has hailed his captain, Lionel Messi, as the best football player ever to set foot on a pitch. Scaloni spoke at his post-match interview after Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in the World Cup final on Sunday. The match against Spain is expected to be Messi’s last i

item.title: Senior lawyer urges NBA president-elect to unite Nigeria’s divided Bar
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: "The Bar is currently polarised along several lines—generational, ideological and, at times, personal," he said. The post Senior lawyer urges NBA president-elect to unite Nigeria’s divided Bar appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: LPG imports jumped by 1400% to 1.5KT/D in June
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Liquefied Premium Gas (LPG) importation soared by 1400 percent in June, the latest data released by the Nigerian Midstream and Downstream Petroleum Regulatory Authority, has shown. The post LPG imports jumped by 1400% to 1.5KT/D in June appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: 2027: APC guber candidates may lose some of these northern states — Primate Ayodele
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The leader of INRI Evangelical Spiritual Church, Primate Elijah Ayodele has revealed some states that APC governors, including incumbent and candidates, may lose in the 2027 elections. Primate Ayodele, in a statement signed by his media aide, Osho Oluwatosin, made it known that the APC cannot win al

item.title: Spain become first nation to hold men’s, women’s World Cup titles simultaneously
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Spain becomes the first nation to simultaneously hold both the men’s and women’s FIFA World Cup titles, marking a historic achievement in football.
Read More: https://punchng.com/spain-become-first-nation-to-hold-mens-womens-world-cup-titles-simultaneously/

item.title: US recorded zero major security incidents during World Cup – FBI
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The FBI Director announced zero major security incidents during the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the US, praising coordinated efforts for the historic event.
Read More: https://punchng.com/us-recorded-zero-major-security-incidents-during-world-cup-fbi/

item.title: China Is Exporting 20% Fewer Magnets to US Despite Trade Truce
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China’s shipments of rare-earth magnets to the US remain well below pre-trade war levels despite last year’s truce, underscoring concerns in the Trump administration about Beijing’s adherence to that agreement.

item.title: World Cup 2026: ‘Same way we’ll beat Tinubu in 2027 polls’ – Sowore congratulates Spain
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The presidential candidate of the African Action Congress, AAC, has congratulated Spain for beating Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final on Sunday. In a post on his verified X handle, Sowore said it will be the same way to beat President Bola Tinubu in the 2027 general elections. DAILY POST re

item.title: Burna Boy makes history with performance at 2026 World Cup Final Halftime Show [VIDEO]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian musician, Damini Ogulu, popularly known as Burna Boy, has made history with his performance at the first-ever FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Show on Sunday. The Nigerian singer was the only African artiste to perform at the show, setting a new record as the first from the continent to g

item.title: Bay Bridge's full brother set for first start at Windsor
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: All eyes will be on Windsor at 6.50pm when Bay Bridge's brother Castlemont makes his first start in the Godolphin blue for Charlie Appleby.

item.title: Hungarian GP: UK schedule and how to watch on Sky Sports
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Formula 1 hits the track for the final time before its summer break at one of the most popular venues on the calendar as the world championship fight rolls on to the Hungarian Grand Prix, live on Sky Sports.

item.title: Cameroon’s porous defences: How gold worth billions escapes to Dubai
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Barely 5% of Cameroon’s gold production is thought to pass through legal channels. Yet the rest still leaves the country, much of it through its international airports. Part 2 of our investigation into a security system riddled with holes.

item.description: #MondayMood #FreshStart #NewWeekEnergy #SoftPush #DailyDrive #KickstartandHustleGood Morning.....Love is the first brick in every relationship. item.description: Arsenal are weighing up a move for John Stones as a replacement for the injured William Saliba, Chelsea hold formal talks over Maxence Lacroix, plus more.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The accident occurred around 3:45 pm and involved four young male occupants, according to officials of the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC), and eyewitnesses at the scene.

item.title: Burna Boy, Shakira smash World Cup record as ‘Dai Dai’ crosses 800 million streams
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian Grammy-winning singer, Burna Boy and Colombian superstar, Shakira have broken a FIFA World Cup music record after their official 2026 tournament anthem, “Dai Dai,” surpassed 800 million combined streams across Spotify and YouTube Music. The achievement comes after the song amassed more than

item.title: Somalia: Somaliland Finance Ministry Unveils 2027 Budget Priorities to Parliament
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Horn Diplomat] Hargeisa -- Somaliland's Ministry of Finance and Economic Development has begun formal consultations with lawmakers on the preparation of the country's 2027 national budget, outlining key fiscal priorities and reaffirming its commitment to a transparent, rules-based budget process.

item.title: Mozambique: 900,000 People in Cabo Delgado Need Humanitarian Assistance
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Agenzia Fides] Quelimane -- A new alarm has been raised over the humanitarian situation in Mozambique, particularly in the province of Cabo Delgado.

item.title: Egypt: Egypt Pledges to Fast-Track Joint Strategic Projects
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Daily News] Dar es Salaam -- EGYPTIAN President Abdel Fattah El-Sisi has pledged to accelerate the implementation of a series of joint economic projects with Tanzania as the two countries seek to deepen trade, investment and regional connectivity.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Suspected Boko Haram/ISWAP insurgents have reportedly abducted the wife of a pastor and her three children in Mobbar Local Government Area of Borno State. The post Suspected Boko Haram terrorists abduct pastor’s wife, three children in Borno appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

Torres was the hero for Spain when he collected substitute Nico Williams’ header to beat Argenti

The post Oluyole APC primary crisis dee

item.title: Kenya: President Ruto's Website Restored As Government Rules Out Data Breach
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- President William Ruto's official website has been restored after a cyberattack forced the government to temporarily take the platform offline, ending nearly a day of disruption that raised concerns over the security of Kenya's digital infrastructure.

item.title: Ogun governorship: Alake delivers verdict on PDP’s Adebutu
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Presenting Adebutu to members of the Egba Traditional Council, Oba Gbadebo praised the PDP standard bearer for his character, pedigree, and commitment to public service.

item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Andy Burnham takes over as Britain’s seventh prime minister in a decade. Discover his urgent challenges, cabinet appointments, and plans for the UK.

item.title: Spain beat Argentina to win World Cup for second time
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Spain beat Argentina 1-0 after extra time in the World Cup final. The Spanish have been crowned champions for the second time in their history. Ferran Torres scored the only goal of the match.

item.title: South Africa: Intelligence Trap - Explosive Affidavit Exposes How Former Police Minister Mchunu Neutralised Inspector General Fazel
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Daily Maverick] Inspector General Imtiaz Fazel's explosive affidavit reveals a troubling power dynamic orchestrated by sidelined police minister Senzo Mchunu, highlighting serious challenges to oversight within South Africa's intelligence framework.

item.title: Nigeria: Terrorists Deploying Drones, Crypto to Deepen Attacks in Nigeria - UN
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [This Day] The United Nations (UN) has warned that terrorist groups operating in northern Nigeria and across the West African and Sahel region are becoming increasingly sophisticated, deploying drones, advanced communications technology and cryptocurrencies to strengthen their operations.

item.title: Senegal’s President Bassirou Faye elected ECOWAS Chairman
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Senegal’s President, Bassirou Diomaye Faye, has been elected Chairman of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS), succeeding Sierra Leone’s President Julius Maada Bio. item.title: Zimbabwe: Zanu-PF Holds Countrywide 'Thank You Rallies' to Appreciate Support for CAB3
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [New Zimbabwe] ZANU PF at the weekend held "CAB3 Thank You Rallies" in all the country's 10 provinces to recognise and appreciate its supporters who rallied behind the recently assented Constitutional Amendment Bill Number 3 into law.

item.title: President Faye Of Senegal Emerges ECOWAS Chairman
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: President Bassirou Diomaye Faye of Senegal is the new Chairman of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS).He succeeds Sierra Leone’s President, Julius Maada Bio.

item.description: The Guardian goes on the ground with Brazilian forces fighting to eradicate mining that has destroyed protected areas of Indigenous landThe jerry-built bordello at the foot of Vila Cururu, an illegal gold-mining settlement at the heart of Brazil’s most desecrated Indigenous territory, was a hive of

item.title: Kenya’s nuclear energy ambitions meet fierce local resistance
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: A sustained campaign by environmentalists and resistance from local communities threaten Kenya’s plan to break ground on its first nuclear power plant next year. Does the government have the political will to see off this anti-nuclear wave?

item.title: Kenya: Kenya's Rift Valley Residents Reach Breaking Point - 'The Lake Moved Into My House'
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [RFI] With Kenya's Lake Baringo and Lake Bogoria rising for months now, following above-average rainfall across the Rift Valley over recent years, this summer has seen them - and the communities around them - reach tipping point.

item.title: Trump: Latest strikes on Iran ‘in honour’ of killed US military personnel
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US President Donald Trump said the latest strikes on Iran were carried out 'in honour' of killed US military personnel.

item.title: China Solar Stocks Rise After Beijing Issues Consumption Tax
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Shares of most Chinese solar and major battery makers rose on Monday after Beijing unveiled a consumption tax on solar cells and lithium batteries, raising hopes it will hasten industry consolidation, particularly in the oversupplied solar sector.

item.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Mixed as Investors Weigh Iran Tensions
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Emerging-market stocks and currencies were mixed as investors weighed rising tensions between the US and Iran, as well as a technology-led selloff that rattled global markets last week.

item.title: AI productivity revolution faces privacy test as digital assistants become more personal
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Artificial Intelligence(AI) systems capable of understanding users’ calendars, emails, documents and financial records are transforming workplace productivity by proactively managing read more AI productivity revolution faces privacy test as digital assistants become more personal

item.title: US releases footage of ninth consecutive night of strikes on Iran
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The US military carried out its ninth consecutive night of strikes on Iran.

item.title: Surviving on a soldier’s N100,000 salary
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The federal government’s decision to double the minimum monthly salary of soldiers from N49,000 to N100,000 has raised a fundamental question about the value placed on those defending the country. The April report of the National Bureau of Statistics’ (NBS) Cost of a Healthy Diet (CoHD) shows that t

item.title: WORLD IN BRIEF: ECOWAS backs Nigeria-Morocco pipeline,US-Iran conflict claims US soldier life, Uganda halts school trips and other stories
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: US soldier killed in Iraq as Iran, US exchange fresh attacks A US service member has been killed and another read more WORLD IN BRIEF: ECOWAS backs Nigeria-Morocco pipeline,US-Iran conflict claims US soldier life, Uganda halts school trips and other stories

item.title: Democracy’s sweet billions
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: At the dawn of this democracy, masked musician, Lagbaja, looked at the value of a million naira and asked in disbelief: “Ṣé mílíọ̀n pọ̀ tó yẹn ni?” (Is a million naira really that much?) Today, he would be speechless—perhaps even songless. The post Democracy’s sweet billions appeared first on Tribu

item.title: Palm Climbs to Near One-Month High as Iran Conflict Boosts Crude
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Palm oil rose to the highest in nearly a month, tracking renewed strength in crude and other vegetable oil prices as the US and Iran exchanged escalating military strikes.

item.title: Spain’s world champions head home to million-strong Madrid welcome
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Spain’s World Cup champions are set to receive a hero’s welcome in Madrid, with a million fans expected to greet the team after their victory. Get the deta
Read More: https://punchng.com/spains-world-champions-head-home-to-million-strong-madrid-welcome/

item.title: Tearful Scaloni uncertain of Argentina future after World Cup loss
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Manager says it would be difficult to assemble another squad capable of reaching two consecutive World Cup finals.

item.title: 1 agency, 2 heads: Tinubu’s directive doesn’t stand, says Wike’s ally
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The embattled Executive Secretary of the Border Communities Development Agency (BCDA), Dr Dakorinama Alabo George, has said that President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s directive to appointees, who aspired for elective positions, to resign ahead of primaries, “does not stand.” Tinubu had directed all his appo

item.title: Business, entrepreneurship and business entrepreneurship
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: BUSINESS, entrepreneurship, and business entrepreneurship are closely related concepts that play a vital role in economic growth, innovation, wealth creation, and societal development. The post Business, entrepreneurship and business entrepreneurship appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: RBI Intervenes to Support Rupee as Currency Nears Record Low
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: India’s central bank stepped into the foreign exchange markets to support the rupee after the currency weakened toward a record low as a renewed surge in crude prices weighed on sentiment.

item.title: FG to raise N729bn bond to clear power sector debts, restore liquidity
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Federal Government is set to raise about N729 billion through a second bond issuance to settle verified legacy debts read more FG to raise N729bn bond to clear power sector debts, restore liquidity

item.title: Niger APC primary crisis pits Bago against ex-Gov Bello
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The outcome of the All Progressives Congress (APC) primaries ahead of the 2027 general elections, coupled with lingering grievances among stakeholders, has worsened the party’s internal crisis in Niger State. The alleged imposition of candidates by Governor Mohammed Umaru Bago during the just-conclu

item.title: Women’s tennis players must take one-time gene test for eligibility
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Players will need to take a test ​for the SRY gene, which helps to determine biological sex, the WTA says.

item.title: Senate’s red line on the pardoning of terrorists
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE Senate’s unequivocal rejection of the policy of rehabilitating and reintegrating repentant terrorists is a welcome pronouncement that resonates profoundly with the national mood. The post Senate’s red line on the pardoning of terrorists appeared first on Tribune Online.
